div.head: The Chargé in Iran (Engert) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 44. Some 15 high ranking Persian Government officials attended the Legation’s Fourth of July reception. Among them Minister for Foreign Affairs who stayed over an hour, several other Cabinet Officers and the President of the Parliament. They explained that others had been unavoidably detained by signing of treaty with IraqFor the treaty and protocol between Iran and Iraq regarding boundary, signed at Teheran, July 4, 1937, see League of Nations Treaty Series, Vol. cxc, p. 241. same afternoon and by preparations for official dinner same evening. I gained the impression that the Government had definitely decided to resume normal friendly relations with this Legation.
